plan.title: PLUGIN-TRUTH: Single PluginRegistration Source of Truth
plan.boundary: packages/opencode/src/plugin/ and packages/plugin/src/ — plugin identity, registration store, error hierarchy, hook dispatch, crash quarantine
plan.consumer_purpose: Eliminates split truth across parallel data structures (PluginHealthStore + CapabilityRegistry), makes pluginId non-optional, adds typed errors, hook timeouts, hook identity context, and immutable dispatch

root_causes.hypothesis: Plugin identity is split across optional pluginId (ListedPlugin), implicit index tracking (hooks[i] ↔ registrations[i]), and separate health/security stores that mutate independently
evidence: ListedPlugin type at index.ts:82 uses pluginId?: string
State holds parallel arrays: hooks: Hooks[], registrations: PluginRegistration[] — indexed by same i
Crash counter persisted to PluginHealthStore (JSON file) then synced to CapabilityRegistry (Ref<Map>) at lines 407-410
tool/registry.ts:421 accesses pluginId via `(p as any).pluginId` — type-unsafe

root_causes.hypothesis: ALWAYS_ALLOWED_HOOKS grants config and auth hooks capability-free access, but these hooks receive full secrets/config objects — a privilege escalation risk
evidence: hook-map.ts:29-33: ALWAYS_ALLOWED_HOOKS = Set{dispose, config, auth, provider}
hook-dispatch-guard.ts:47: bypasses all checks for ALWAYS_ALLOWED_HOOKS
But config hook receives full config (including secrets) via `(hook as any).config?.(configForPlugin)` at line 338
auth hook receives full Provider hook context via provider/auth.ts:115-118

root_causes.hypothesis: Crash quarantine reset on persistSuccess creates an oscillation vector where crash→succeed→crash→succeed evades the 3-crash threshold indefinitely
evidence: persistSuccess at lines 413-420 resets crashCount to 0 on any successful hook dispatch
A plugin that alternates crash/resume across 6 hooks will never quarantine

root_causes.hypothesis: No hook timeout means a hung plugin hook (infinite loop, network stall) blocks all subsequent plugin hooks in the serial for-loop
evidence: trigger() at lines 425-460 iterates hooks sequentially in a for loop
Each fn(input, output) is called via Effect.tryPromise with no timeout mechanism
Effect.timeout is never applied to hook invocations

fixes.description: Make pluginId required — introduce PluginIdentity struct and remove Optional from ListedPlugin

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts — ListedPlugin type, list() function, trigger() function
packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts:421 — remove `(p as any).pluginId`, use `p.pluginId` directly
packages/opencode/src/provider/auth.ts:115 — x.auth?.provider uses hooks from list(), no change needed
packages/opencode/src/provider/provider.ts:1254 — reads direct from hooks, no change needed
packages/opencode/test/plugin/trigger.test.ts — any existing tests mocks may need identity field

risks: MINOR: Legacy plugins that don't export an `id` — already rejected by resolvePluginId() in shared.ts:304 which throws for file plugins without id
MINOR: The list() function currently does Object.assign(hook, {pluginId}) — needs to be changed to {pluginId, identity}
MINOR: External consumers of ListedPlugin type (from plugin package re-exports) need the new field
validation: Typecheck: bun typecheck from packages/opencode — ListedPlugin must always have pluginId: string
Test: Update trigger.test.ts with an identity asserted on listed plugins
Smoke: Full provider layer build — verify auth/config hook resolution still works

fixes.description: Unify PluginHealthStore (persisted JSON file) + CapabilityRegistry (Ref<Map>) into single PluginRegistration store. PluginRegistration becomes the merged truth. Persistence delegates to health store JSON file on write.

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts — ~40 lines removed (health store), ~20 lines changed (State, persistCrash, persistSuccess, unquarantine, getCrashStatus)
packages/opencode/src/plugin/capability/registry.ts — setCrashCount() and setQuarantined() removed (now on PluginRegistration directly)
packages/opencode/src/plugin/capability/types.ts — PluginSecurityState import replaced with PluginRegistration
packages/opencode/src/plugin/capability/enforcer.ts — checkCapability uses registry.get() which now returns PluginRegistration
packages/opencode/src/plugin/capability/hook-dispatch-guard.ts — shouldDispatch() uses registry.get()

risks: MAJOR: Removing setCrashCount/setQuarantined from Registry changes its public API — but grep confirms NO external callers exist outside index.ts (only internal sync at lines 407-410)
MEDIUM: The loadPluginHealthStore() JSON file path changes — old health store files become stale but harmless (new path writes PluginRegistration[])
MEDIUM: Effect Ref.update pattern for capability switching needs careful migration — Registry.get() currently returns PluginSecurityState, needs to return PluginRegistration or a compatibility shim
MINOR: Two internal callers use capabilityRegistry.setCrashCount/setQuarantined — replace with direct array mutation on registrations[i]
validation: Typecheck: bun typecheck from packages/opencode — all Registry references updated
Test: Verify crash counter increments in registrations[i].crashCount (not separate JSON store)
Test: Verify quarantine state is read from registrations[i].quarantined at load time
Smoke: Multiple plugin load → trigger crash → verify quarantine persisted across process restart

fixes.description: Add typed PluginError hierarchy extending NamedError. Five error classes: PluginLoadError, PluginHookError, PluginConfigError, PluginCapabilityDeniedError, PluginDisposeError.

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/plugin/errors.ts — NEW file, 0 downstream breakage
packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts — change publishPluginError(), log.error calls, all catch blocks
packages/opencode/src/plugin/loader.ts — wrap load errors in PluginLoadError at call site (line 288-295 in index.ts)
packages/opencode/src/plugin/shared.ts — resolvePluginId() already throws TypeError; now can throw PluginLoadError
tests_to_run: packages/opencode/test/plugin/trigger.test.ts — verify error types on hook failure

risks: MINOR: Existing catch blocks pass `error: err` to log.error — changing to structured errors requires no log format change (they're JSON-serializable)
MINOR: loader.ts's public API keeps `error: unknown` for backward compat; wrapping happens at the consumer (index.ts)
validation: Typecheck: bun typecheck from packages/opencode
Test: Trigger a hook that throws → catch block produces PluginHookError
Test: Load a nonexistent plugin → PluginLoadError with stage='install'

fixes.description: Add hook timeout (default 30s) with per-hook override. Uses Effect.timeout on the fn(input, output) promise.

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts — trigger() function (lines 425-460)
packages/opencode/src/plugin/capability/hook-map.ts — add HOOK_TIMEOUT_OVERRIDES (optional)

risks: MINOR: Long-running hooks (e.g., heavy config processing on startup) may need timeout overrides. The override map is the escape hatch.
MINOR: Test flakiness if timeout values are too tight — use generous values in tests (e.g., 500ms for test hooks, 30s default for real)
validation: Test: Plugin with hook that sleeps 40s → timeout → crash counted
Test: Plugin with hook overridden to 60s → 40s hook succeeds
Smoke: 30s default timeout does not fire on normal hooks

fixes.description: Add hook identity context — every hook dispatch carries (pluginId, hookName, capability) in log messages and errors.
fixes.before:
// index.ts:447-450 — log.error with inline pluginId
log.error("plugin hook error", { pluginId, hook: name, error: err })

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts — trigger() function
packages/opencode/src/plugin/capability/hook-dispatch-guard.ts — shouldDispatch() already receives hookName+pluginId, no change

risks: MINOR: HookInvocation type is internal-only, no external API change
validation: Test: Trigger a hook that fails — log output includes {pluginId, hookName, capability}
Typecheck: no external consumers affected

fixes.description: Narrow ALWAYS_ALLOWED_HOOKS from {dispose, config, auth, provider} to {dispose, provider}. Config and auth hooks must now pass through capability checks when dispatched via trigger().
fixes.before:
// hook-map.ts:29-33
export const ALWAYS_ALLOWED_HOOKS = new Set(["dispose", "config", "auth", "provider"])

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/plugin/capability/hook-map.ts — 1 line change
packages/opencode/src/plugin/capability/hook-dispatch-guard.ts — no code change, just semantic change

risks: LOW: No existing trigger('config') or trigger('auth') calls exist in codebase. Verified by grep across all 14 consumer files.
LOW: If a future test or plugin dispatches trigger('config', ...) it will be denied unless the plugin has config.read capability.
validation: Test: Call trigger('dispose', ...) — still allowed (always-allowed)
Test: Call trigger('provider', ...) — still allowed (always-allowed)
Test: Call trigger('config', ...) with a plugin lacking config.read — denied
Test: Call trigger('auth', ...) with a plugin lacking secrets.access — denied
Smoke: Full integration test suite — no config/auth hooks break

fixes.description: Add explicit Plugin.dispose(id) API that calls a specific plugin's dispose hook and removes it from the hooks/registrations arrays.

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts — Interface type, layer body

risks: MEDIUM: Splice from parallel arrays (hooks, registrations) must stay synchronized — but we're in an Effect and the arrays are guarded by InstanceState already.

MINOR: If a hook is being dispatched in trigger() while dispose() is called, the for-loop's cached length may cause out-of-bounds access. Mitigation: dispose sets a tombstone (hook = null) instead of splicing.
MINOR: External callers holding references to ListedPlugin from list() will have stale references — this is pre-existing behavior.
validation: Test: Load plugin, call dispose(id), verify hooks.length decreased by 1
Test: Call dispose on nonexistent pluginId — no-op
Test: Dispose plugin with failing dispose hook — hook still removed, error logged
Smoke: Full cleanup at scope exit via addFinalizer still works for remaining plugins

fixes.description: Fix crash quarantine — count persists across sessions, not reset on persistSuccess. Adds a separate crashWindow counter that resets on success but crashTotal persists.

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts — persistCrash, persistSuccess, layer initialization

risks: MINOR: Plugins with high crashCount from previous bugs will quarantine on first load after update — this is the intended behavior
MINOR: unquarantine() must reset both crashCount and crashWindow to 0
validation: Test: Plugin crashes 3 times → quarantined
Test: Plugin crashes 2 → succeeds 1 → crashes 2 more → quarantined (window is 4)
Test: Plugin crashes 5 times over 2 sessions → still quarantined on second session
Test: unquarantine() → crashCount=0, crashWindow=0, quarantined=false

fixes.description: Make hook output immutable per-plugin by shallow-cloning the output object before passing it to each hook. One plugin's mutation cannot corrupt the next plugin's input.

affected_files: packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts — trigger() function

risks: MINOR: structuredClone has overhead for large objects — but output objects in hooks are small ({args}, {output}, {messages}, {env})
MINOR: structuredClone may fail on objects with functions/circular refs — fallback to spread handles this

MINOR: Callers that rely on mutating output (e.g., hook sets output.enabled = true to signal state) will silently break. Mitigation: use the clonedOutput for dispatch but merge result back into original? NO — that's the bug. The ENTIRE output is passed to each hook independently; hooks should not rely on prior hook mutations. This is the fix.
validation: Test: Plugin A mutates output.foo = 'corrupted', plugin B sees original output.foo
Test: structuredClone fallback works with circular references
Smoke: All existing trigger() callers — verify no hook depends on cross-plugin mutation

plan.rollback_plan: Revert each fix in reverse order (I→A). Each fix is independently revertible since they target different code regions. Fix-B is the riskiest — if rollback needed, restore separate health store and Registry setCrashCount/setQuarantined methods.

migration_phases.name: Identity + Store Unification
migration_phases.rationale: PluginIdentity and unified PluginRegistration are prerequisites for all other fixes. These change the core data model.

migration_phases.name: Error Hierarchy + Hook Infrastructure
migration_phases.rationale: Typed errors, timeouts, and hook identity build on the unified store but don't change behavior.

migration_phases.name: Security Hardening + Crash Fix + Immutability
migration_phases.rationale: Behavioral changes that need the unified store in place for proper quarantine tracking.

key_decisions.decision: Keep hooks[] and registrations[] as parallel arrays instead of a single array of {hook, registration} objects
key_decisions.reason: Minimizes edit surface — all existing code indexes hooks[i]/registrations[i] by position. Unifying into a single array would touch every loop in trigger(), list(), the config dispatch loop, the finalizer, and the event subscription setup. Assessment: defer to a future cleanup phase.

key_decisions.decision: Do NOT add config.read and secrets.access capabilities to HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP for config/auth hooks
key_decisions.reason: These hooks are NEVER dispatched through trigger() — they are called directly in the layer initialization code. Adding them to the map would be dead code. The narrowing of ALWAYS_ALLOWED_HOOKS is defense-in-depth for any future trigger() calls.

key_decisions.decision: crashWindow resets on process start (not persisted) while crashCount persists
key_decisions.reason: Prevents a plugin with 100 historical crashes from being permanently unusable across updates. crashWindow = 0 on each start gives the plugin a fresh chance while crashCount provides diagnostics.

api_change: packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts — Interface type gains dispose() method
